title: rhodiola rosea subsp. atropurpurea
intro: Rhodiola rosea subsp. atropurpurea, also known as the Arctic rhodiola or Roseroot, is a subspecies of the Rhodiola rosea plant, a perennial flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include adaptogenic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects, allowing it to help the body cope with stress and fatigue. The health benefits of Rhodiola rosea subsp. atropurpurea include improved mental performance, reduced anxiety and depression, enhanced physical performance, and improved sleep quality. Rhodiola rosea subsp. atropurpurea is commonly prepared as a tea, capsule, or tincture, and can also be consumed as a supplement, making it easily accessible for those looking to harness its medicinal properties.
